title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 42 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Spring Festival (Chinese New Year)
festivals.description: China's most important festival celebrating the lunar new year with family reunions, fireworks, dragon dances, and traditional feasts lasting 15 days. It's a time of cultural renewal and the busiest travel period in China.

festivals.name: Harbin Ice and Snow Festival
festivals.description: The world's largest ice sculpture festival featuring massive illuminated ice palaces, sculptures, and winter activities in the frozen Songhua River. It transforms the city into a magical winter wonderland.

festivals.name: Lantern Festival
festivals.description: Celebrated on the 15th day of the lunar new year, featuring lantern displays, dragon dances, and traditional sweet dumplings. It marks the end of Spring Festival celebrations.

festivals.name: Qingming Festival
festivals.description: A national holiday for tomb sweeping and honoring ancestors with family gatherings and outdoor activities marking the start of spring. Families visit gravesites with offerings and pay respects.

festivals.name: Pear Flower Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of blooming pear blossoms in Lüliang with cultural performances and agricultural displays showcasing local harvests. The white flowers create stunning landscapes across the valleys.

festivals.name: Dragon Boat Festival
festivals.description: A traditional festival featuring dragon boat races, zongzi (glutinous rice dumplings), and cultural performances commemorating poet Qu Yuan. Communities across China hold colorful racing competitions on waterways.

festivals.name: Midi Music Festival
festivals.description: One of Asia's largest rock and pop music festivals attracting international and Chinese artists with multiple stages and performances. It's a major event for music lovers seeking contemporary Asian culture.

festivals.name: Longsheng Rice Terrace Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the stunning Longsheng Rice Terraces with minority cultural performances, agricultural traditions, and harvest festivities. Visitors experience the Zhuang and Yao peoples' vibrant traditions.

festivals.name: Zhongshan Lantern Festival
festivals.description: A colorful celebration featuring elaborate lantern displays, traditional Cantonese performances, and local food festivals. The festival celebrates the region's rich cultural heritage.

festivals.name: Naadam Festival
festivals.description: The 'Olympics of the steppes' featuring traditional Mongol sports including horse racing, wrestling, and archery with colorful festivities and nomadic culture displays. It's the largest cultural event in Inner Mongolia.

festivals.name: Mid-Autumn Festival
festivals.description: A major lunar calendar festival celebrating family reunion with mooncakes, lanterns, and traditional performances under the full moon. Families gather to give thanks and enjoy festive meals.

festivals.name: Chrysanthemum Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of blooming chrysanthemums in traditional gardens with flower exhibitions, cultural performances, and art displays. Kaifeng is famous for hosting one of China's oldest flower festivals.

festivals.name: National Day Golden Week
festivals.description: China's most important national holiday celebrating the founding of the People's Republic with parades, festivities, and mass tourism across all regions. Major attractions offer special celebrations and performances.

festivals.name: Shoton Festival (Yogurt Festival)
festivals.description: A traditional Tibetan Buddhist festival featuring opera performances, giant thangka displays, and yogurt consumption celebrating the end of summer monasteries' retreats. It's a vibrant blend of religious and cultural traditions.

festivals.name: Potala Palace Pilgrimage Festival
festivals.description: A spiritual gathering where pilgrims visit the sacred Potala Palace in Lhasa for religious devotions and traditional ceremonies honoring Tibetan Buddhism. The white-washed walls are beautifully maintained during this period.

festivals.name: Kashgar Sunday Bazaar
festivals.description: A vibrant weekly market showcasing Uyghur culture, traditional crafts, spices, textiles, and livestock trading with authentic Central Asian atmosphere. It's one of the world's most colorful traditional markets.

festivals.name: Urumqi International Grand Bazaar Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Silk Road culture featuring traditional performances, crafts markets, and multicultural cuisine representing Xinjiang's diverse ethnic groups. It attracts visitors from across Central Asia.

festivals.name: Mount Tai International Tourism Festival
festivals.description: A festival celebrating one of China's sacred mountains with hiking events, cultural performances, and traditional rituals. Mount Tai holds significant spiritual importance in Chinese culture.

festivals.name: Yuanyang Rice Terrace Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the UNESCO-listed Yuanyang Rice Terraces with Hani minority culture, traditional practices, and stunning landscape photography opportunities during golden season. The terraces reflect sunlight creating magical patterns.

festivals.name: Lijiang Old Town Festival
festivals.description: A cultural event in the ancient Naxi settlement featuring traditional music, dance, and ceremonies preserving the ethnic heritage of southwestern China. The cobblestone streets host performances and cultural exhibitions.

festivals.name: Torch Festival
festivals.description: An ancient Yi, Bai, and Naxi ethnic festival featuring torch processions, wrestling competitions, and bonfires celebrating prosperity and warding off evil spirits. It's a spectacular display of fire and cultural traditions.

festivals.name: Shuangxi Lantern Festival
festivals.description: A traditional festival featuring elaborate lantern displays, folk performances, and temple fairs in ancient Shuangxi village. The illuminated lanterns create magical nighttime scenes.

festivals.name: Kite Flying Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of traditional Chinese kite culture with international competitions and displays in the ancient city of Weifang. The skies fill with colorful handmade kites of various designs.

festivals.name: West Lake International Expo
festivals.description: A major cultural and business exposition at the scenic West Lake featuring art exhibitions, international performances, and traditional Chinese culture showcases. The beautiful lakeside setting enhances the experience.

festivals.name: Qingyuan Longmu Lake Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Guangdong's natural beauty with water sports, cultural performances, and local cuisine festivals at the scenic Longmu Lake. Traditional Cantonese activities highlight regional heritage.

festivals.name: Fujian Tea Culture Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Fujian's famous oolong and black tea traditions with tea tastings, cultural exhibitions, and traditional ceremonies. The region is renowned for producing some of China's finest teas.

festivals.name: Pingtan Blue Tears Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the rare bioluminescent plankton phenomenon creating glowing blue waters at Pingtan Beach. Visitors experience this magical natural light show during calm nights.

festivals.name: Shaanxi History Culture Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of ancient Chinese civilization featuring archaeological exhibitions, traditional performances, and historical reenactments. Xi'an's rich imperial heritage is showcased through various cultural events.

festivals.name: Terracotta Warriors Lighting Festival
festivals.description: Evening illuminations and cultural performances at the famous Terracotta Warriors site creating a spectacular nighttime experience of ancient China's most iconic discovery. The lighting design showcases the warriors beautifully.

festivals.name: Zhangye Danxia Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the colorful geological formations with photography contests, hiking events, and cultural performances showcasing northwestern China's dramatic landscapes. The rainbow mountains attract artists and photographers worldwide.

festivals.name: Mogao Caves Cultural Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the ancient Buddhist art and Silk Road heritage with exhibitions, lectures, and cultural performances at this UNESCO World Heritage site. The caves contain thousands of years of Buddhist artistic traditions.

festivals.name: Qinghai Lake Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of the world's highest salt lake featuring sports competitions, cultural performances, and environmental awareness activities with Tibetan and Mongolian traditions. The scenic lake setting is spectacular during summer.

festivals.name: Kumbum Monastery Festival
festivals.description: An important Tibetan Buddhist festival featuring butter lamp displays, religious ceremonies, and spiritual gatherings at one of Tibetan Buddhism's most sacred monasteries. Thousands of pilgrims participate in the celebrations.

festivals.name: Stone Forest Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Yunnan's unique landscape featuring Yi ethnic performances, torch festivals, and hiking events in this UNESCO World Heritage geological site. The dramatic karst formations create an otherworldly setting.

festivals.name: Guilin Lantern Festival on Li River
festivals.description: A stunning display of traditional lanterns floating on the Li River with cormorant fishing demonstrations and classical music performances. The lantern-lit landscape reflects China's poetic traditions.

festivals.name: Yangshuo Climbing Festival
festivals.description: An international rock climbing competition and festival in the scenic karst landscape of Yangshuo attracting climbers worldwide. The dramatic limestone peaks provide world-class climbing challenges.

festivals.name: Luoping Rape Flower Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of blooming yellow rape flowers covering vast hillsides creating stunning natural carpets with cultural performances and photography opportunities. One of China's most spectacular springtime landscapes.

festivals.name: Huanglong Temple Fair
festivals.description: A traditional temple fair at the sacred Huanglong site featuring religious ceremonies, folk performances, and cultural celebrations blending Buddhism with local traditions. The travertine terraces provide a sacred natural backdrop.

festivals.name: Jiuzhaigou Golden Autumn Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of autumn colors in the stunning Jiuzhaigou Valley with hiking events, photography contests, and cultural performances as the landscape transforms to gold and red. The scenic valley is at its most photogenic during this season.

festivals.name: Zhaoqing Seven-Star Crags Festival
festivals.description: A cultural celebration featuring boating, traditional performances, and scenic tourism at the ancient limestone peaks forming the shape of the Big Dipper constellation. The karst landscape offers stunning natural beauty.

festivals.name: Anhui Poetry and Tea Culture Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of classical Chinese poetry and Anhui's famous Huangshan tea with cultural salons, poetry readings, and tea tastings in historic mountain settings. The festival honors literati traditions of ancient China.

festivals.name: Suzhou Garden Lantern Festival
festivals.description: An enchanting display of traditional lanterns illuminating the classical gardens of Suzhou with performances and cultural exhibitions celebrating the region's artistic heritage. The UNESCO gardens are transformed by dramatic lighting.
